title: phegopteris difformis
intro: Phegopteris difformis, also known as the maidenhair fern or the variable fern, is a type of fern native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. Its health benefits include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing fever and alleviating menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Phegopteris difformis are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices to utilize its medicinal properties.
